# Database Architecture

## Storage Model

cartographer uses offline-only JSON file persistence. There is no network database, no SQL engine, and no ORM. All data is stored in a single flat JSON file at:

```
~/.cartographer/graph.json
```

The file contains a serialised `Graph` object with two top-level arrays: `nodes` and `edges`. Pretty-printed with two-space indentation (`JSON.stringify(graph, null, 2)`).

`lowdb` is listed as a dependency in `package.json` but is **not used** in the current source code. Persistence is handled entirely by Node.js built-in `fs` functions (`readFileSync`, `writeFileSync`, `mkdirSync`).

## Entity Inventory

### Node

Represents a knowledge-graph entry (concept, idea, or fact).

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | `string` (UUID v4) | Unique identifier generated via `crypto.randomUUID()` |
| `title` | `string` | Human-readable name; used as the primary lookup key (case-sensitive) |
| `body` | `string` | Free-form text content |
| `tags` | `string[]` | Zero or more classification labels |
| `createdAt` | `string` (ISO 8601) | Creation timestamp |
| `updatedAt` | `string` (ISO 8601) | Last-modified timestamp |

### Edge

Represents a directed relationship between two nodes.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `from` | `string` (Node.id) | Source node UUID |
| `to` | `string` (Node.id) | Target node UUID |
| `label` | `string` (optional) | Human-readable relationship descriptor |
| `weight` | `number` | Relationship strength; always set to `1` at creation [?] |

### Graph

Top-level container serialised to disk.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `nodes` | `Node[]` | All nodes in the graph |
| `edges` | `Edge[]` | All edges in the graph |

## Relationships

Edges are directed (`from` â†’ `to`) and reference nodes by `id` (UUID), not by `title`. Node lookup during edge creation resolves titles to UUIDs at write time (`graph.nodes.find(n => n.title === fromTitle)`). There is no foreign-key enforcement at the storage layer â€” referential integrity is maintained only in application code at the time `link()` is called.

- One node can have many outgoing edges (one-to-many via `Edge.from`).
- One node can have many incoming edges (one-to-many via `Edge.to`).
- Multiple edges between the same pair of nodes are not explicitly prevented [?].
- Self-referencing edges (a node linking to itself) are not explicitly prevented [?].

## Persistence Mechanism

All reads and writes go through two private functions in `src/store/graph.ts`:

**`load(): Graph`**
- Checks for file existence via `existsSync(DATA_PATH)`.
- Returns `{ nodes: [], edges: [] }` if the file does not exist (first run).
- Otherwise reads the file synchronously with `readFileSync` and parses JSON.

**`save(graph: Graph): void`**
- Ensures the `~/.cartographer/` directory exists via `mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })`.
- Writes the entire graph as formatted JSON via `writeFileSync`.
- Each mutation (add node, add edge) performs a full read-then-write cycle; there is no partial update or append mechanism.

The `DATA_PATH` is resolved at module load time:

```
join(process.env.HOME ?? "~", ".cartographer", "graph.json")
```

There are no migrations, no schema versioning, no seed data, and no backup mechanism.
